story.logline: A founder admits that his costliest automation failed because the team had never agreed on the process it was meant to automate.
story.dramaticQuestion: Can one concrete failure replace vague automation confidence with a usable operating rule?

beats.title: Symptom
beats.purpose: Prove the failure with one observable operational detail.

beats.title: Rule
beats.purpose: Leave the viewer with a testable operating principle.

characters.name: Arun
identityLock: South Asian man in his late thirties

scenes.title: One honest take
scenes.purpose: Deliver a credible operational lesson without performance polish.

shots.title: The rule we learned late
shots.intent: Hold attention through specificity, natural delivery, and one earned shift in framing.
shots.subject: Arun seated at the end of a marked-up worktable facing slightly off lens
shots.action: he admits the failed automation, shows the contradictory handoff notes, then states the rule
shots.environment: working studio office with real cables, paper notes, and an inactive monitor

camera.movement: nearly locked camera with a slow six-inch push beginning only when the handoff notes appear
camera.framing: eyes on upper third, hands and notes enter lower frame without cropping
camera.focusBehavior: stable eye focus with a brief deliberate transfer to the notes

lighting.primarySource: large window key from camera left
lighting.motivation: remaining evening daylight with one desk practical

physics: paper notes bend under his grip and settle on the table
chair and body weight remain grounded
focus transfer follows the notes without breathing wildly

beats.action: Arun says the automation cost three weeks and made every handoff worse
he lifts two contradictory process notes and explains that both teams thought the other one approved refunds
camera begins its slow push as he states the rule: do not automate a process until two humans can run the same version of it
after the final phoneme he seals his lips once and holds the same relaxed open-eyed pose through the final frame

continuityLocks: same face, hair, overshirt, watch, and seated posture
same two marked-up notes remain in his hands after reveal
same window and desk-lamp direction throughout
imperfectionAnchors: one natural breath before the self-incriminating detail
subtle chair fabric tension when he leans forward

performance.basePosture: seated and grounded, facing slightly off lens until the closing rule
performance.eyeLine: stable near-lens attention with no teleprompter scanning
performance.emotionalExpressionSource: eyes and brows, with one restrained lean at the rule

audioTrack.spokenText: We spent three weeks automating a refund handoff and made every refund slower. The embarrassing part was not the model. Sales thought support approved the refund; support thought sales did. We had automated an argument. Our rule now is simple: if two humans cannot run the same version of a process, software should not run it yet.
soundDesignDirectives: one paper rustle at the proof beat

shots.dialogue: We spent three weeks automating a refund handoff and made every refund slower. The embarrassing part was not the model. Sales thought support approved the refund; support thought sales did. We had automated an argument. Our rule now is simple: if two humans cannot run the same version of a process, software should not run it yet.
